title: Toji as a pilot
wikitext:
[]: Toji as a pilot?             Quote Post by Güf Trüp » Fri Feb 15, 2013 11:21 pm This is just a "what if" thread about how he'd effect the show were he to somehow become a permanent Eva Pilot. Would he provide a more stabilizing element to the show? Would his presence prevent further disasters, or cause new ones? The best difference I could come up with was the effects he'd have on Shinji's pysche. The fact that he's got his best friend by his side through it all would definitely at least ease some of his emotional break down, and I dare say having to kill Kaworu would be significantly less painful for him. This would also put him in a better condition for EOE and provide another pilot to fight the Mass Produced Evas. While there's a high chance things would still turn out "eh..." in the end, could Toji indirectly save humanity?

[]:             Quote Post by Lance of LoL » Sat Feb 16, 2013 12:24 am My friend and I have said it "Why can't he be the main character" What you can easily find looking at Toji is that he's a lot closer to the typical Shonen hero than Shinji, and considering that Unit-03 itself was considered a state of the art Eva, he would have definitely been a powerful force on the battle field. Looking at the events that followed, his presence may have tipped the scale against Zeruel, causing a lot less damage, though equally possible that he would've went down along with Asuka and Rei, and potentially that his stabilizing influence on Shinji might've doomed them all as without that rage that Shinji let loose they might've failed against Zeruel. Arael would be unlikely to change significantly. Armisael, I've not the faintest idea, that thing was a monster even among Angels. Fighting Tabris, it's a question of whether or not Kaworu would've stolen 02 still if 03 was an option. Even so, we're looking at a tough fight. It wasn't until Kaworu allowed it that Shinji was able to kill him. So adding another Eva into the mix would be unlikely to change things. Finally, the MP Evas, with 03 on hand, they might've saved Asuka, as between the two of them they likely could've beaten the JSSDF even faster and so long as one of them wasn't on emergency power they could've taken the MP Evas. As for the mental side of things, Toji's psychological influence might've pulled Shinji's head out of his ass a lot quicker. So over all, I'd say yes, Toji could've saved the world if the series wasn't all about piling crap on Shinji 'til he can't take it anymore.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Sat Feb 16, 2013 12:25 am I'd imagine he would help Shinji's psyche somewhat. Shinji would never have tried to quit being a pilot, and He wouldn't have hit rock bottom in the EoE continuity. Also, i give your username a DENOUTTADEN!

[]:             Quote Post by Güf Trüp » Sat Feb 16, 2013 12:49 am You raised some nice points there, but what you said about the Tabris fight ad me thinking. Lance of LoL wrote: Fighting Tabris, it's a question of whether or not Kaworu would've stolen 02 still if 03 was an option. Even so, we're looking at a tough fight. It wasn't until Kaworu allowed it that Shinji was able to kill him. So adding another Eva into the mix would be unlikely to change things.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Sat Feb 16, 2013 1:14 am Zeruel is the Arm of God and can only be overcome by the irresistible power of screaming for mommy. Toji would have no chance.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Feb 16, 2013 3:43 am If fan art is any guide, the a conseqence of Touji being a pilot is that Hikari will be as well -- probably in the successfully commissioned 03 and 04 respectively.

[]: Re: Toji as a pilot?             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sat Feb 16, 2013 3:44 am Güf Trüp wrote: The best difference I could come up with was the effects he'd have on Shinji's pysche. The fact that he's got his best friend by his side through it all would definitely at least ease some of his emotional break down,

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Sat Feb 16, 2013 5:39 am Mr. Tines wrote: If fan art is any guide, the a conseqence of Touji being a pilot is that Hikari will be as well -- probably in the successfully commissioned 03 and 04 respectively.

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Sat Feb 16, 2013 10:00 am Mr. Tines wrote: If fan art is any guide, the a conseqence of Touji being a pilot is that Hikari will be as well -- probably in the successfully commissioned 03 and 04 respectively.

[]: G Gundam makes everything Awesome. BD             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sat Feb 16, 2013 10:04 am The Series didn't need Toji, it needed Domon Kasshu. Pretty sure he would have been more Asuka's type than Mr. screams-a-lot (though more so than Toji, hard to say).

[]: Re: Toji as a pilot?             Quote Post by user-02 » Sat Feb 16, 2013 10:34 am pwhodges wrote: I'm not sure about that. Remember how badly Touji took the news that he had been chosen as a pilot, in contrast to his former brash persona; he might not have had the inner strength to give Shinji any real support at all in the end.

[]:             Quote Post by amitakartok » Sat Feb 16, 2013 11:11 am The way I see it, the Toji/Kaworu dilemma would have one of two outcomes: when Kaworu reveals his true nature... Toji gets pissed at Kaworu apparently toying with Shinji and kills Kaworu, creating a rift between himself and Shinji. Toji hesitates at killing what he's told is an Angel but looks completely like a human, resulting in Shinji having to do the killing. Afterward, Toji starts to understand just how much suffering Shinji is going through.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Feb 16, 2013 6:16 pm The thing with Touji and Kensuke is, they kinda look like archetypic shonen leads (Touji being the classic "tough guy with protector complex" (compare: Kurosaki Ichigo etc) and Kensuke the archetypical "big dreamer", like, say, Naruto. Kensuke is most blatant since he's basically a toned down 21st century version of Anno's previous protagonist Jean), but Kensuke doesn't get chosen and Touji didn't get any heroic beginner's luck, no, the one with the plot-relevant parents, the crucial remote control and the prophecies about him happens to be that insecure, timid kid with preciously little stress tolerance. Because, not everyone whose parents need a handy can for the rampaging demon fox is automatically gonna have the personality of a messiac archetype, so to speak. Most likely not, since plot relevant parents have this unfortunate tendency to die or dissapear of the map. --- As for, what if Touji stayed, yeah, he would probably be forced to mature/ didn't take the revelation too well - Yeah, he's a tough 14 year old with a healthy sense of responsibility, but he's still a 14 year old, and eps 17/18 showed an appropiate reaction. At least he potentially has his father, grandfather and sister to support him. so he'd probably be able to take it all with a lot more grace that Shinji did - The first battle experience would still have him mildly traumatized, because, realism, even a tough kid is still a kid, y chromosome or not, and Rei and Asuka are indefferent because they were conditioned for most of their lives. Touji had a mostly normal lives with siblings and stuff. Not sure what having him in the team would do to Asuka. Might speed up her downward spiral since they don't get along and he's yet another "average joe" having a status that should make her Elite, but could it really be much worse than what actually happened to the poor girl? I can see his stay having a positive influence on Rei since they seemed to be on their way to sort of... mh, maybe "become friends" is too much, but they were sure getting along in the rooftop scene/ has a serious conversations about their feelings towards the situation there. I can easily see Rei ending up integrated into Shinji's circle of friends if not for the Bardiel fiasco, I don't think she'd have a problem with doing typical boy activities or be too girly in a disruptive, dungeonmasters-girlfriend-way. Shinji wouldn't face anyone he knew from school after the whole thing, so it not happening would not only mean keeping a best friend available, but the retention of that whole support structure at least until armisael, or longer if the presence of another EVA changes the outcome of the battle. Something like, "Ayanami, eject, the two of us can deal with that thing!" But let's not be too optismistic. Kaworu... hm, that could create an interesting situation, because, I guess Touji was always special to Shinji because he's one person wgho couldn't stand him at first, and he manaed to prove himself to him, and for someone who always fears being hated/ agonizes over the question of "what do I do if someone doesn't like me", that's mayor milestone/ important memory. But they haven't got that much in common - With the "baka trio" it's probably an issue of them complimenting each other's more/less rational sides, but a sensitive guy who's into classical music, an avid military geek and a basketball-playing class-clown-y type probably isn't a combination that you see hanging out together to often - And then, enter Kaworu, who shares several hobbies with Shinji and generally has a lot in common with him - I guess Touji would find him weird at first and politely remind him of what personal space is, but he seemed to get along ok with Rei by ep 17, so no real reason why they can't make the trio a quartett, dun think Kensuke or Kaworu would mind much, but I can see Kaworu wanting to spend some time alone with his favorite Lillim specimen, so that might leave room for that classic highschool plot of some kid making a new BFF and their old buddies wondering if they'll still get enough of their time. Not sure where the angel reveal would fit into this - If Kaworu had become a member of their group by this point, Touji would react like a classic shonen protagonist - lots of anger, but outweighed by a desire for explanation, "Was it all a lie, etc", "Why weren't you honest with us". Kaworu may not get why a human would see a paradox between planning to annihilate them and spending time with them unless their was intentional betrayal - Kaworu probably sees it as enjoying em humans while they last. Not sure if Touji could actually off him - probably not, he'd still be a kid with even less battle experience than Shinji. Heck, would Shinji be able to off Kaworu under such circumstances? No Bardiel incident also means that this would be his first experience with actually "shooting the dog" - and it would be worse than ep 18, that was only a theoretical risk to get Touji injured during the fight, not being told to willingly, certainly kill him as it would be if that first-time experience happened by then. So who'd take him out, assuming he can't do it himself and Asuka is still out of the window? Rei? Without some Mecha's assistence? They should be evenly matched at best... And a surviving ReiII in Zerogoki? Hm. There's the offensice capacity she wouldn't have without mechas, but the thing with Rei II is, she, too, couldn't quite pull the trigger on something with Touji inside, so whether she could actually do it is not certain... but more likely than with Shinji, she may still ultimately have pulled that trigger if Bardiel hadn't noticed her frirst. Also, how long would it take for Touji to be actually usable? As far as we know, Shinji is an extremely exceptional case - Misato mentions that " even Rei" needed 7 months to properly synch with her EVA, implying that the completely human Asuka needed even longer. It may be some peculiarity of Shinji's physical or mental makeup, or simply the fact that EVA 01, being a copy of Lillith, is more similar to and thus more compatible with humans, but it would be unlikely that Touji would get the synching done any faster than Asuka who was probably above him in the list of candidates for a reason.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Sun Feb 17, 2013 3:57 am Kendrix wrote: Also, how long would it take for Touji to be actually usable? As far as we know, Shinji is an extremely exceptional case - Misato mentions that " even Rei" needed 7 months to properly synch with her EVA, implying that the completely human Asuka needed even longer. It may be some peculiarity of Shinji's physical or mental makeup, or simply the fact that EVA 01, being a copy of Lillith, is more similar to and thus more compatible with humans, but it would be unlikely that Touji would get the synching done any faster than Asuka who was probably above him in the list of candidates for a reason.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Sun Feb 17, 2013 5:51 am amitakartok wrote: Toji hesitates at killing what he's told is an Angel but looks completely like a human, resulting in Shinji having to do the killing. Afterward, Toji starts to understand just how much suffering Shinji is going through.

[]:             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Sun Feb 17, 2013 8:24 am CJD wrote: Kensuke who wants very much to be chosen.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Sun Feb 17, 2013 8:26 am Giji Shinka wrote: THIS is what i've been thinking through the entire series, why not let Kensuke pilot the freaking eva?? If the school where Shinji and the others went were full of potential eva-pilots, why not let Kensuke, who is mentally STABLE and who WANTS to pilot. Man...... NERV sure is full of idiotic people, or the plot just demands that it must be these three: Shinji, Rei, Asuka....

[]:             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Sun Feb 17, 2013 9:29 am Dream wrote: I assume you don't know how the Evangelions work.

[]:             Quote Post by Güf Trüp » Sun Feb 17, 2013 12:43 pm Simply piloting an Eva takes a serious toll on your mental health, so pretty much any one (except maybe Kaworu) would get broken at least some what. But Shinji and Asuka are in significantly worse condition for the job then someone like Toji would be. Toji lacks the severe past traumas and accompanying mental disorders that they do, so while there's no doubt he'd be broken by Eva, I feel like he'd be a little bit better off then his co-workers.

[]:             Quote Post by Giji Shinka » Sun Feb 17, 2013 2:13 pm Güf Trüp wrote: Simply piloting an Eva takes a serious toll on your mental health, so pretty much any one (except maybe Kaworu) would get broken at least some what. But Shinji and Asuka are in significantly worse condition for the job then someone like Toji would be. Toji lacks the severe past traumas and accompanying mental disorders that they do, so while there's no doubt he'd be broken by Eva, I feel like he'd be a little bit better off then his co-workers.
